structured_payload_candidate.title: Supply Fan Start/Stop
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Supply fan runs during Cooldown, Setup, or Occupied Mode. If perimeter VAV-reheat boxes exist, fan also runs during Setback or Warmup Mode.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: System mode is Cooldown, Setup, or Occupied Mode; or if perimeter VAV-reheat boxes exist, also Setback or Warmup Mode.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Supply fan shall run when system is in the specified modes.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Supply fan shall run when system is in the Cooldown Mode, Setup Mode, or Occupied Mode. If there are any VAV-reheat boxes on perimeter zones, supply fan shall also run when system is in Setback Mode or Warmup Mode (i.e., all modes except unoccupied).

structured_payload_candidate.title: Relief Fan P-Only Control Loop
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Multiple-Zone VAV Air-Handling Unit with relief fans
structured_payload_candidate.summary: A single P-only control loop for each pressure zone maintains building pressure at 12 Pa setpoint. Loop enabled when any supply fan in the pressure zone is proven ON; disabled with output set to zero otherwise.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Any supply fan within the pressure zone is proven ON.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: A single P-only control loop for each pressure zone shall maintain the building pressure at a setpoint of 12 Pa (0.05 in. of water) with an output ranging from 0% to 100%. The loop shall be enabled when any supply fan within the pressure zone is proven ON. The loop is disabled with output set to zero otherwise.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Building Pressure Control Loop for Return Fan (Direct Building Pressure)
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Multiple-Zone VAV Air-Handling Unit with return fan and direct building pressure control
structured_payload_candidate.summary: A single P-only control loop for each pressure zone modulates to maintain building pressure at 12 Pa. Loop enabled when supply and return fans are proven ON and minimum OA damper is open. Exhaust dampers closed otherwise. From 0-50% loop output, modulate exhaust dampers; from 51-100%, reset return-fan discharge static pressure setpoint from RFDSPmin to RFDSPmax.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Supply and return fans for any unit within the pressure zone are proven ON and the minimum outdoor air damper is open.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: A single P-only control loop for each pressure zone shall modulate to maintain the building pressure at a setpoint of 12 Pa (0.05 in. of water) with an output ranging from 0% to 100%. The loop shall be enabled when the supply and return fans for any unit within the pressure zone are proven ON and the minimum outdoor air damper is open. The exhaust dampers shall be closed with loop output set to zero otherwise. All exhaust damper and return fan static pressure setpoints for units in an associated pressure zone shall be sequenced based on building pressure control loop output signal, as shown in Figure 5.16.10.5. a. From 0% to 50%, the building pressure control loop shall modulate the exhaust dampers from 0% to 100% open. b. From 51% to 100%, the building pressure control loop shall reset the return-fan discharge static pressure setpoint from RFDSPmin at 50% loop output to RFDSPmax at 100% of loop output.
configurable_values: Building pressure setpoint: 12 Pa (0.05 in. of water)
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: A single P-only control loop for each pressure zone shall modulate to maintain the building pressure at a setpoint of 12 Pa (0.05 in. of water) with an output ranging from 0% to 100%. The loop shall be enabled when the supply and return fans for any unit within the pressure zone are proven ON and the minimum outdoor air damper is open. The exhaust dampers shall be closed with loop output set to zero otherwise. All exhaust damper and return fan static pressure setpoints for units in an associated pressure zone shall be sequenced based on building pressure control loop output signal, as shown in Figure 5.16.10.5. a. From 0% to 50%, the building pressure control loop shall modulate the exhaust dampers from 0% to 100% open. b. From 51% to 100%, the building pressure control loop shall reset the return-fan discharge static pressure setpoint from RFDSPmin at 50% loop output to RFDSPmax at 100% of loop output.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Freeze Protection Stage 2
structured_payload_candidate.summary: If SAT drops below 3.3°C for 5 minutes, fully close both economizer and minimum OA dampers for 1 hour and set Level 3 alarm. After 1 hour, resume minimum OA ventilation and enter Stage 1.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Supply air temperature drops below 3.3°C (38°F) for 5 minutes.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: If the supply air temperature drops below 3.3°C (38°F) for 5 minutes, fully close both the economizer damper and the minimum outdoor air damper for 1 hour and set a Level 3 alarm noting that minimum ventilation was interrupted. After 1 hour, the unit shall resume minimum outdoor air ventilation and enter the previous stage of freeze protection (see Section 5.16.12.1).
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: If the supply air temperature drops below 3.3°C (38°F) for 5 minutes, fully close both the economizer damper and the minimum outdoor air damper for 1 hour and set a Level 3 alarm noting that minimum ventilation was interrupted. After 1 hour, the unit shall resume minimum outdoor air ventilation and enter the previous stage of freeze protection (see Section 5.16.12.1).

structured_payload_candidate.title: Freeze Protection Stage 3
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Upon freeze-stat signal or if SAT drops below 3.3°C for 15 minutes or below 1°C for 5 minutes, shut down supply and return/relief fans, close OA damper, open cooling coil valve to 100%, energize CHW pump system, send two HHW plant requests, modulate heating coil to maintain higher of SAT or MAT at 27°C, and set Level 2 alarm. Requires software reset.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Freeze-stat signal OR supply air temperature drops below 3.3°C (38°F) for 15 minutes OR below 1°C (34°F) for 5 minutes.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Upon signal from the freeze-stat (if installed), or if supply air temperature drops below 3.3°C (38°F) for 15 minutes or below 1°C (34°F) for 5 minutes, shut down supply and return/relief fan(s), close outdoor air damper, open the cooling-coil valve to 100%, and energize the CHW pump system. Also send two (or more, as required to ensure that heating plant is active) heating hot-water plant requests, modulate the heating coil to maintain the higher of the supply air temperature or the mixed air temperature at 27°C (80°F), and set a Level 2 alarm indicating the unit is shut down by freeze protection. If a freeze-protection shutdown is triggered by a low air temperature sensor reading, it shall remain in effect until it is reset by a software switch from the operator’s workstation.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Upon signal from the freeze-stat (if installed), or if supply air temperature drops below 3.3°C (38°F) for 15 minutes or below 1°C (34°F) for 5 minutes, shut down supply and return/relief fan(s), close outdoor air damper, open the cooling-coil valve to 100%, and energize the CHW pump system. Also send two (or more, as required to ensure that heating plant is active) heating hot-water plant requests, modulate the heating coil to maintain the higher of the supply air temperature or the mixed air temperature at 27°C (80°F), and set a Level 2 alarm indicating the unit is shut down by freeze protection. If a freeze-protection shutdown is triggered by a low air temperature sensor reading, it shall remain in effect until it is reset by a software switch from the operator’s workstation.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Filter Pressure Drop Alarm
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Level 4 alarm when filter pressure drop exceeds the larger of alarm limit or 12.5 Pa for 10 minutes and airflow exceeds 20%. Alarm limit varies with airflow: DPx = DP100 * (x)^1.4.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Filter pressure drop exceeds the larger of the alarm limit or 12.5 Pa (0.05”) for 10 minutes when airflow (expressed as a percentage of design airflow or design speed if total airflow is not known) exceeds 20%: Level 4. The alarm limit shall vary with total airflow (if available; use fan speed if total airflow is not known) as follows: DPx = DP100 (x)^1.4 where DP100 is the high-limit pressure drop at design airflow and DPx is the high limit at the current airflow rate x (expressed as a fraction).
configurable_values: Constant threshold: 12.5 Pa (0.05 in. of water)
structured_payload_candidate.fault_condition: Filter pressure drop exceeds the larger of the alarm limit or 12.5 Pa (0.05”) for 10 minutes when airflow exceeds 20%.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Filter pressure drop exceeds the larger of the alarm limit or 12.5 Pa (0.05”) for 10 minutes when airflow (expressed as a percentage of design airflow or design speed if total airflow is not known) exceeds 20%: Level 4. The alarm limit shall vary with total airflow (if available; use fan speed if total airflow is not known) as follows: DPx = DP100 (x)^1.4 where DP100 is the high-limit pressure drop at design airflow and DPx is the high limit at the current airflow rate x (expressed as a fraction).
